title: Baku City Circuit
sub: Monaco meets Monza on the shores of the Caspian: a Tilke street circuit pairing a claustrophobic uphill castle section with one of the longest flat-out blasts in F1. Walls everywhere, and a safety car almost guaranteed.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: Heavy braking into the 90° Turn 1, the Turn 2-3 lefts onto a short DRS straight, then the wide-open boulevard through the fast Turn 5.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: The lap’s slow, claustrophobic heart: the uphill climb into the UNESCO old town and the notorious Turn 8 pinch, just 7.6m wide, where cars have wedged in the wall.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: Off Turn 16 the track goes flat-out for over 2km along the Caspian seafront, past the finish line to Turn 1, one of the longest full-throttle runs in F1.

tyreAlloc: Softest in the range · C3 / C4 / C5
stratNote: Low grip on a dusty, part-time street surface keeps thermal wear modest, so it is a natural one-stopper. The real strategy variable is the safety car, which appears in the vast majority of Baku races and turns a cheap stop into free track position.
spots: End of the 2.2km flat-out run + DRS, from ~350 to ~90 km/h. The biggest braking zone and the prime overtaking spot.
A late dive into the 90° left after the short Turn 2 DRS straight.
A tow off the final corners sets up the slipstream down the long blast to Turn 1.
otNote: The enormous slipstream down the 2.2km run means cars stream past into Turn 1, but the narrow castle section makes following there almost impossible. Big speed, big walls, and a safety car that reliably reshuffles the order.
corners: Hard stop from ~350 km/h at the end of the long blast + DRS. The prime overtaking spot and a lap-one flashpoint.
Quick left onto a short DRS straight.
Another right-angled left with a well-used escape road; a strong secondary passing spot.
Quick, near-flat left along the wide-open boulevard.
Medium left starting the climb toward the old town.
Tight left as the walls close in on the approach to the castle.
The castle pinch: just 7.6m wide, uphill and blind. Cars have literally wedged in the wall here.
Claustrophobic low-speed flick through the UNESCO old town.
Tight left at the top of the castle section.
Quick kinks dropping back toward the seafront.
Medium left linking the old town to the long run.
The pivotal corner: a good exit launches the 2.2km flat-out run. Exit speed is everything.
Gentle seafront kinks taken flat, slipstreams building all the way.
Final left (DRS 1 detection) firing cars onto the start/finish straight and the run to Turn 1.
geo: A Tilke street circuit that splices a claustrophobic Monaco-style old-town section onto Monza-fast boulevards. Finding a wing level for both is the weekend’s puzzle.
Through the medieval UNESCO old town the track squeezes to just 7.6m at Turn 8, uphill and blind, the narrowest point in F1. No room, no margin.
From Turn 16 the cars run flat-out for over 2km along the Caspian seafront to Turn 1, hitting ~355 km/h. Huge slipstreams make it a passing playground.
Walls line every corner and the surface is low-grip and dusty, so Baku throws a safety car in the vast majority of its races. Strategy is built around when, not if.
